what_it_does: A generated data blob that models the entire design-system directory as a graph: a `nodes` array (one entry per file/folder with id, name, path, parent, type, ext, role, size, defines[], uses[]) and an `edges` array (relationships keyed by `kind`/`type`, including 'contains' tree edges and the loads/uses resolution edges). It is the single data source consumed by system/system-map.html to draw the treemap and wiring.
observations: Oversized generated blob — the on-disk file is exactly 262144 bytes (256KB) and the view ends mid-string inside the edges array, indicating it is capped/truncated at read time; not hand-authored
Each node carries: id, name, path, parent (null for root), type (file|folder), ext, role (doc/card/token/archive/ingest/folder/...), size (bytes or null for folders), defines[], uses[]
Edges seen are 'contains' relations (e.g. screenshots → screenshots/atomicity-vi.png) with both kind and type set to 'contains'; the consumer also expects loads and uses:<global> edges
Roles observed in the sample: doc, card, token, archive, ingest, folder — matching the role vocabulary in system-map.html's RC/ROLE tables
Top-level entries include CLAUDE.md, DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md, README.md, SKILL.md, colors_and_type.css, styles.css, tonal-zoning.css, plus _archive-v1, _ingest, screenshots folders
system-map.html notes this file is the OUTPUT of system/build-system-map.js and is polled for regeneration; it may also carry an `encoding` block (DATA.encoding) the visualiser reads for live colours
standouts: It is the materialised model behind the visualiser: a flat node list plus typed edges that encode both directory containment and code resolution in one document
conventions: Generated artifact (build-system-map.js output), not edited by hand — matches the design folder's 'edit data/source, not generated files' rule
Role + size metadata on every node lets downstream lenses size/colour nodes without re-reading source
concerns: The file as stored is 256KB and appears truncated mid-edge in this view; if the on-disk artifact is actually cut off it would be invalid JSON and fail the consumer's JSON.parse (cannot confirm truncation is real vs a read cap from this file alone)
notes: The generated graph model (nodes + contains/loads/uses edges with role/size metadata) that system-map.html renders; produced by build-system-map.js, not hand-edited.
questions: Is the 256KB file genuinely complete valid JSON, or is it truncated (the visible tail ends mid-string)?
Does this file include the `encoding`/`globals` blocks the HTML consumer references, and what is their shape?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its role as the data source: it is exactly the node/edge model system-map.html indexes and draws.
apparent_themes: Codebase-as-data / self-model (the directory expressed as a queryable graph)
Generated single source feeding a projection (data vs view separation)
intention_signals: It exists to capture the whole repository's structure and wiring as machine-readable data so a separate view can project it many ways without re-parsing the source tree.
contribution: Contributes the concrete graph representation of a codebase — files/folders as nodes with role+size, and containment+resolution as typed edges — that a fused system would keep as the data substrate for any directory/dependency visualisation.
summary_for_embedding: Generated JSON graph model of the ConceptV design-system directory: nodes (files/folders with role, size, defines/uses) and edges (contains/loads/uses), consumed by system-map.html.
intention_for_embedding: To represent the entire codebase's structure and wiring as machine-readable data so the system-map view can render and re-project it.
